problem.title: Balanced Binary Tree
problem.description: Given a binary tree, determine if it is height-balanced.
assumptions: A height-balanced binary tree is one where every node's left and right subtree heights differ by at most 1.

example.explanation: Every node's left and right subtrees differ in height by at most 1.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Recursive with Height [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Perform a single post-order DFS that returns the subtree height if balanced, or -1 as a sentinel to signal imbalance. By checking balance and computing height in one pass, we avoid the O(n log n) cost of a naive two-pass approach. The key insight is to propagate the failure sentinel upward immediately so no extra work is done once an imbalance is detected.
